attacking_style: Opportunistic rather than aggressive — capitalizes on deadlocked GC groups or stage-hunting scenarios rather than initiating attacks. Most effective when racing for stages without GC pressure, avoiding direct confrontation with pure climbers.
team_tactics: Often given co-leadership or split focus (BMC with Porte example), diluting dedicated domestique support. Better deployed as TT specialist in non-GC squads where he can target selective stages without sustained climbing defense obligations.
terrain_preference: Time trials and moderately difficult stages where TT power compensates for climbing limitations. Exposed on sustained summit finishes and early-stage climbs where pure climbers can apply pressure. Vulnerable in technical/nervous peloton positioning.
vulnerability: Significant form volatility across season — multiple abandonments and early-climb drops despite previous strong results at same races. Poor close-quarters positioning leads to crashes/contact with roadside objects. Cracks under sustained GC pressure on climbs.
key_patterns: Form decline trajectory evident: dropped early on climbs at races where previously finished 2nd (Dauphine)
Crashes/mechanicals in technical sections: face injuries from road signs twice, punctures, finale crashes
Performs better without GC pressure: strong when stage-hunting versus defending GC positions
Early-stage vulnerabilities: abandoned/dropped within 100km multiple times despite pre-race leadership status
Team leadership ambiguity: BMC split focus between multiple riders rather than committing hierarchy
form_trajectory: negative: Clear decline from 2017-2019. Strong 2019 Dauphine followed by crashes and 2020 Dauphine showed decisive early-climb weakness. Abandonments and drops increased in frequency through career span analyzed.
